Purpose: Detect and quantify post-mortem damage patterns in aDNA reads; rescale base qualities to mitigate damage
Specifics: The standard aDNA authentication tool. Profiles characteristic C->T (5') and G->A (3') misincorporations and read-length distributions. Rescales BAM quality scores so that downstream callers down-weight likely-damaged bases. First step in any aDNA pipeline.
Issues and warnings: Damage profiles can be confounded by USER/UDG-treated libraries (which remove the signal). On extremely low coverage or short reads the rescaling becomes noisy.

Purpose: Damage-aware genotype calling, glikelihoods and variant calling for aDNA
Specifics: Joint estimation of post-mortem damage rate and genotype likelihoods within a single framework. Provides recalibrated quality scores accounting for damage and sequencing errors estimated per sample. Outputs are usable directly by ANGSD / PCAngsd / ngsRelate.
Issues and warnings: Computationally heavier than the mapDamage rescale-and-call pipeline; benefits are largest for very low coverage and heterogeneous damage profiles.

Purpose: Filter reads by post-mortem damage score to enrich for endogenous aDNA
Specifics: Computes a per-read PMD score combining damage frequency and length; useful when contamination is suspected and one wants to retain only reads with strong aDNA signature.
Issues and warnings: Aggressive PMD filtering reduces coverage; trade-off between contamination control and statistical power.

Purpose: Estimate mitochondrial contamination and recover endogenous mtDNA consensus
Specifics: Bayesian decomposition of the mtDNA reads into endogenous + contaminant fractions, with a contamination-aware mtDNA consensus. The standard mtDNA contamination check.
Issues and warnings: mtDNA-only - genome-wide nuclear contamination needs different tools (hapCon, contamDE, ANGSD's X-contamination estimator).

Purpose: Estimate nuclear contamination in male aDNA samples by haplotype copying on chrX
Specifics: Companion to hapROH from the same lab. Treats chrX in males as effectively haploid and estimates the fraction of reads coming from a different haplotype using a reference panel. Accurate at very low coverage. Female samples with long ROH can also be analysed.
Issues and warnings: Reference panel choice matters for non-European samples. Female non-ROH samples are not directly handled.

Purpose: Detect runs of homozygosity in low-coverage ancient and modern genomes
Specifics: Reference-haplotype copying HMM that calls ROH down to ~0.3x coverage on the 1240k panel. Outputs feed into demographic-history inference (HapNe-ROH, conservation Ne estimates) and identification of close-kin parents in ancient populations.
Issues and warnings: Requires a reference panel matched to the ancestry of the target. Performance below 0.3x is unreliable. Pseudo-haploid / 1240k input recommended.

Purpose: Pedigree-degree estimation from low-coverage / pseudo-haploid ancient DNA
Specifics: Pseudo-haploid pairwise mismatch approach designed for aDNA. READv2 (2024) refines normalisation, error handling and reporting; the recommended default for routine aDNA kinship up to the 3rd degree.
Issues and warnings: Pseudo-haploid framework loses sensitivity below ~0.05x. Background relatedness in the population biases the normalisation - use a clearly unrelated reference set.
Reference: (Monroy Kuhn et al., 2018; Alacamli et al., 2024)

Purpose: Ultra-low-coverage relatedness inference from aDNA shotgun data
Specifics: Estimates 1st- and 2nd-degree relatedness from coverage as low as ~0.026x - extending kinship analysis to the low-coverage screening stage of aDNA projects. Best paired with READv2 / lcMLkin for cross-validation.
Issues and warnings: Limited to close-kin (1st-2nd degree). Background allele-frequency mis-specification inflates false positives at very low coverage.

Purpose: Genotype-likelihood-based kinship inference for aDNA, with explicit IBD-segment modelling
Specifics: Models IBD-segment lengths along chromosomes (rather than just summary kinship) to disambiguate degree-2 relationships. Designed specifically for aDNA challenges (low coverage, damage, pseudo-haploid input).
Issues and warnings: Newer than READ / TKGWV2 - benchmark on your own data before relying on close calls. Recombination-map dependent.

Purpose: Maximum-likelihood relatedness from low-coverage sequencing
Specifics: Genotype-likelihood-based ML estimator for IBD coefficients (k0, k1, k2). Earlier alternative to ngsRelateV2; still appears in benchmarks alongside READ / TKGWV2 / KIN.
Issues and warnings: Less actively maintained than ngsRelateV2; consider that as the default unless you specifically need lcMLkin's parameterisation.

Purpose: Compute SFS, FST, theta, Tajima's D, ancestry, X-chromosome contamination, etc. from BAM files
Specifics: The genotype-likelihood toolkit underlying most aDNA popgen pipelines.
Issues and warnings: Reference-allele bias matters for very low coverage and for damaged reads - use --remove_bads, base-quality recalibration and damage-aware filters.

Purpose: Phasing and imputation of ancient genomes against modern reference panels
Specifics: Already in Table 1 - flagged here because GLIMPSE2 is now widely used to impute aDNA at coverages as low as 0.1x against the 1000G + HGDP panel, expanding what selection / structure analyses can be done on existing aDNA datasets. Pair with QUILT2 for biobank-scale references.
Issues and warnings: Reference-panel ancestry match matters even more for aDNA than for modern data. Aggressive INFO/GP filters are essential.

Purpose: Local-ancestry inference from low-coverage and ancient DNA, including archaic ancestry
Specifics: Already in Table 2 - flagged here because admixfrog is the standard local-ancestry tool for aDNA at low coverage, including archaic introgression. Handles damage and pseudo-haploid input.
Issues and warnings: Coverage and reference-panel calibration dominate accuracy; benchmark on simulated reads.

Purpose: Estimate selection coefficients from temporal allele-frequency data
Specifics: Family of methods that exploit time-stratified ancient samples to estimate selection coefficients without relying on linkage. BMWS and Slattice are MLE-based; ApproxWF and Sr are ABC-based. Recent benchmarks (Tournebize & Chikhi 2026 GBE) recommend using more than one and reporting the agreement.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to time-bin choice, sample-age uncertainty and aDNA error rates. Confidence intervals are often optimistic - bootstrap your own.

Purpose: Full-likelihood selection-coefficient estimation from inferred ARGs and/or aDNA time series
Specifics: Already in Table 2 - flagged here because CLUES2 is the natural ARG-based companion to BMWS / Slattice for joint use of linkage and temporal information. Supports epoch-specific selection coefficients and dominance.
Issues and warnings: ARG quality dominates errors; demographic mis-specification biases s.

Purpose: Multi-locus genotype identity scan applied to time-stratified aDNA
Specifics: Davy et al. (2024) demonstrate that the G12 statistic, run on pseudo-haplotypes, outperforms SweepFinder2 for aDNA selection scans across European Holocene populations. Detects haplotype-based signals despite the difficulty of phasing aDNA.
Issues and warnings: Pseudo-haplotypes are an approximation. Population structure across time periods can confound; stratify by time bin.

Purpose: Genealogy reconstruction including ancient samples as 'ancestral tip' lineages
Specifics: Already in Table 3 - flagged here because Relate, SINGER and Threads now all support time-stratified aDNA samples placed at their radiocarbon dates, enabling joint ARG-based demographic inference and selection scans on combined ancient-modern datasets.
Issues and warnings: Phasing aDNA is hard; pseudo-haploid input degrades branch-length resolution. Sample-date uncertainty propagates.

Purpose: Date tree-sequence nodes using ancient sample tip dates as constraints
Specifics: Already in Table 3 - flagged here because tsdate now incorporates aDNA tip-date constraints and is widely used in unified human ARG analyses (Wohns et al. 2022, Schaefer et al. 2024).
Issues and warnings: Topologies are more reliable than dates; aDNA tip dates are useful but bound only one end of the genealogy.

Purpose: Date admixture events from ancestry-covariance decay in ancient samples
Specifics: Already in Table 3 - flagged here because DATES is the standard tool to date admixture pulses in archaeogenetic studies (Steppe ancestry, Yamnaya, etc.) when both modern and ancient samples are available.
Issues and warnings: Single-pulse model; needs differentiated reference panels and adequate coverage of admixed samples.

Purpose: Test admixture models for ancient samples with explicit rejection
Specifics: Already in Table 3 - flagged here because qpAdm with the rotation strategy (Harney et al. 2021), as implemented in ADMIXTOOLS 2 / admixr, is the standard archaeogenetic admixture-test workflow. Accepts pseudo-haploid data.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to right-population choice; rotation is essential. Pseudo-haploid input loses some power.

Purpose: Determine Y-chromosome haplogroups from low-coverage data
Specifics: Standard tool for calling Y-haplogroups in aDNA studies; outputs ISOGG-compatible labels.
Issues and warnings: Coverage thresholds matter; deep haplogroup resolution requires many called variants.

Purpose: mtDNA haplogroup classification with quality scores
Specifics: Standard mtDNA haplogroup classifier; works with low-coverage consensus sequences from schmutzi or similar.
Issues and warnings: Heteroplasmy and contamination must be handled upstream (schmutzi).

Purpose: End-to-end aDNA processing pipeline (read mapping, damage, contamination, genotype calling)
Specifics: Nextflow-based reproducible pipeline that wraps mapDamage, BWA aln, samtools, GATK, schmutzi, contamMix, etc. The current de-facto standard aDNA processing pipeline alongside PALEOMIX.
Issues and warnings: Defaults are tuned for human aDNA - non-human projects need parameter review.

Purpose: Reproducible aDNA processing (mapping, damage, consensus calling)
Specifics: Earlier than nf-core/eager but still widely used, especially for non-human aDNA. Solid for read trimming, mapping, damage profiling and consensus calling.
Issues and warnings: Less actively developed than nf-core/eager.
